query: I built an AI Agent in n8n and attached an HTTP Request Tool to it. The agent just never calls it — it answers from its own knowledge instead. The tool node is named 'tool1' and I left the description field blank because the URL is right there in the node. The model and memory are wired correctly. What's going on?
expected_behavior: Explain that the agent selects tools ONLY by reading their name and description — it cannot see the node's URL or internal config
Identify both problems: a generic name ('tool1') and an empty description make the tool effectively invisible to the model
Note that this fails silently — there is usually no error, the agent just skips the tool or hallucinates
Recommend a verb-first, specific name (e.g. 'Search customer database', 'Lookup order by ID')
Recommend a real description: what the tool does, when to use it, and what each $fromAI parameter means
Frame tool name + description as part of the prompt / API design, not documentation
Reference TOOLS.md for naming and description patterns
notes: The #1 agent mistake: treating tool name/description as cosmetic. The model picks tools purely from name + description + $fromAI param descriptions; a blank description is a non-tool. Skill must reframe these fields as prompt engineering and give the rename + real-description fix.
